name: API Blueprint
description: API Blueprint — open-source, Markdown-based API description language for designing, documenting, and testing REST APIs.

seo.title: API Blueprint — Developer Documentation / DevRel
seo.meta_description: API Blueprint: API Blueprint — open-source, Markdown-based API description language for designing, documenting, and testing REST APIs.

when_to_use: Markdown-first API design reviewed in pull requests
Mock server generated from Blueprint spec during frontend build
Contract testing between backend and consumers via Dredd
API changelog tracked as diffs to Blueprint files in Git
Human-readable spec shared with external integration partners

pros: Human-readable Markdown syntax lowers authoring friction
Open specification, not tied to one vendor
Tooling ecosystem exists for parsing and mocking
Good fit for design-first API workflows
cons: Industry has largely moved to OpenAPI instead
Fewer modern tools support Blueprint than OpenAPI
Conversion to OpenAPI often needed for integrations
review.verdict: A readable API description format whose moment has passed - OpenAPI is now the safe default.
review.best_for: Existing Blueprint codebases or teams committed to Markdown-first API authoring.
review.not_for: New projects - start with OpenAPI 3.1 for broader tooling and long-term support.
